title: opt_rmdff incorrectly optimises $_DFFSR_PPP_ to $_DFF_P_
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
`yosys -f ilang -p "opt_rmdff; stat" <file>`
```
# Generated by Yosys 0.8+148 (git sha1 95789c6, clang 3.8.0-2ubuntu4 -fPIC -Os)
autoidx 62
attribute \src "sr.v:1"
module \dffsr1
  attribute \src "sr.v:2"
  wire input 2 \arst
  attribute \src "sr.v:2"
  wire input 1 \clk
  attribute \src "sr.v:2"
  wire input 3 \d
  attribute \src "sr.v:3"
  wire output 4 \q
  attribute \src "sr.v:4"
  cell $_DFFSR_PPP_ $auto$simplemap.cc:467:simplemap_dffsr$58
    connect \C \clk
    connect \D \d
    connect \Q \q
    connect \R \arst
    connect \S 1'0
  end
end
```

## Expected behavior

Expecting no optimisation to happen.

## Actual behavior

`$_DFFSR_PPP_` incorrectly optimised to `$_DFF_P_`
```
 Yosys 0.8+152 (git sha1 2ca83005, clang 6.0.0-1ubuntu2 -fPIC -Os)


-- Parsing `dff.ilang' using frontend `ilang' --

1. Executing ILANG frontend.
Input filename: dff.ilang

-- Running command `opt_rmdff; stat' --

2. Executing OPT_RMDFF pass (remove dff with constant values).
Converting $auto$simplemap.cc:467:simplemap_dffsr$58 ($_DFFSR_PPP_) to $_DFF_P_ in module dffsr1.

3. Printing statistics.

=== dffsr1 ===

   Number of wires:                  4
   Number of wire bits:              4
   Number of public wires:           4
   Number of public wire bits:       4
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                  1
     $_DFF_P_                        1
```

The same circuit, described in Verilog, does not get optimised away
Help from @daveshah1 to get this far -- he seems to think that:
> going through Verilog makes it a `\$_DFFSR_PPP_` - the leading \ specifies it as a user defined cell

**Important Note:** Nobody will be able to help you and/or fix the issue if you
do not provide sufficient information for reproducing the problem.
